title: Configure Indentation in the Script Editor
breadcrumbs: Configure Indentation in the Script Editor
text: Configure Indentation in the Script Editor By default, the Script Editor is configured to indent code using a tab character that is equivalent to 4 spaces. If your coding standards or preferences call for the use of spaces or a different tab size, you can change this configuration. Uniface provides the following two ways to configure the indentation settings: By adding the formatting subsection in the ASN file By modifying the widget properties in the INI file Note: Any settings defined in the ASN file always takes precedence over the widget properties defined in the INI file. We strongly recommend that all developers share the same formatting subsection to maintain code consistency. Configuring indentation across multiple installations To configure code indentation for multiple developers, create a formatting subsection in an ASN file, and make sure it is included: ;ASN file [formatting] .. IndentSize = 2 IndentTabs = false .. Copy IndentTabs: Use the tab characters for indentation; one of on (use tabs) or off (use spaces). The default value is on . IndentSize: The size of the indentation in space characters. The default value is 4 . Configuring indentation for a single installation When code consistency is not a concern, developers can define their preference as a widget property in the SourceEdit widget. The INI file allows the use of IndentGuides , which shows a visual guide of the indentation. Since this setting does not affect the ProcScript code, it can only be set in the INI file. It cannot be set in the ASN file. To configure code indentation, edit the Script Editor definition in the usys.ini file: ;usys.ini [widgets] ... SourceEdit=usrcedit(font=procboxfont;language=uniface;indenttabs=off;indentsize=2;indentguides=off) Copy IndentTab: Use the tab characters for indentation; one of on (use tabs) or off (use spaces). The default value is on . IndentSize: The size of the indentation in space characters. The default value is 4 . IndentGuides: To display indentation guides as vertical lines, the default is off .
